name: Josh
role: CS major, beginner network lab builder
backstory: Josh is newly curious about networking and has a strong programming foundation. He has two free computers he can run as Proxmox hosts and a Cisco switch, and wants to learn by building and breaking a real homelab safely.
emotional_triggers: Getting stuck on VLAN or routing concepts without clear visibility
Fear of breaking everything and not knowing rollback steps
Too much theory without hands-on feedback
existing_strengths: Comfort with terminal workflows and documentation
current_situation: Josh has two machines available for Proxmox and one Cisco switch. He wants a practical first roadmap that combines networking fundamentals with scripting/automation.
success_looks_like: In 4-8 weeks, Josh can design a small segmented lab (management + server + guest VLANs), deploy basic services in VMs/containers, verify connectivity with tests, and automate repeatable checks with scripts.

week_1: Install Proxmox on one machine, keep the second as backup/test node
Create a simple network diagram (physical + logical)
Learn and verify: IP/subnetting, gateway, DNS, DHCP
week_2: On Cisco switch: create 2-3 VLANs (mgmt, lab-server, guest)
Configure a trunk to Proxmox and one access port per VLAN
Test inter-VLAN behavior and document expected vs actual results
week_3: Deploy core services in VMs/containers: DNS (Pi-hole/Unbound), DHCP, and a Linux server
Add firewall rules for least privilege between VLANs
Use ping/traceroute/port checks and keep a troubleshooting log
week_4_plus: Write scripts (Python/Bash/PowerShell) for health checks and config backups
Practice change plans: objective, risk, rollback, validation
